classification.reasoning: This log indicates a firewall rule denied a TCP connection attempt to port 22, which is relevant to cybersecurity and may indicate a potential unauthorized access attempt.

investigation.aiSuggestion: A firewall rule has blocked a TCP connection attempt from 10.0.0.240 to 10.0.0.41 on port 22 (SSH). This may indicate a potential unauthorized access attempt or scanning activity targeting SSH services.
investigation.attackVector: Unauthorized SSH access attempt or reconnaissance scan

proposedSteps.title: Review Source Activity
proposedSteps.description: Check recent logs for repeated or similar denied attempts from 10.0.0.240 to identify possible brute-force or scanning behavior.

proposedSteps.title: Validate Firewall Rules
proposedSteps.description: Ensure that firewall rules are correctly configured to block unauthorized SSH access and that no exceptions exist for 10.0.0.240.

proposedSteps.title: Investigate Source Host
proposedSteps.description: Investigate the user and process on 10.0.0.240 responsible for the connection attempt to determine if it is legitimate or malicious.

resolution.executiveSummary: A firewall rule blocked a TCP connection attempt from 10.0.0.240 to 10.0.0.41 on port 22 (SSH), indicating a possible unauthorized access attempt or reconnaissance scan. The resolution plan involves reviewing source activity, validating firewall rules, and investigating the source host to determine intent and prevent recurrence.

immediateActions.title: Review Source Activity
immediateActions.description: Check recent logs for repeated or similar denied attempts from 10.0.0.240 to identify possible brute-force or scanning behavior. This is safe and helps detect ongoing or automated attacks.

immediateActions.title: Validate Firewall Rules
immediateActions.description: Ensure that firewall rules are correctly configured to block unauthorized SSH access and that no exceptions exist for 10.0.0.240. This prevents accidental exposure of SSH and is a safe verification step.

immediateActions.title: Investigate Source Host
immediateActions.description: Investigate the user and process on 10.0.0.240 responsible for the connection attempt to determine if it is legitimate or malicious. This step is necessary to confirm intent and may require coordination with endpoint or user teams.

followUpActions.title: Report and Remediate Source Host if Malicious
followUpActions.description: If the investigation finds malicious activity on 10.0.0.240, isolate the host and perform a full malware scan and remediation.

followUpActions.title: Review SSH Access Policies
followUpActions.description: Audit and update SSH access policies to ensure only authorized hosts can connect to critical systems.

followUpActions.title: Monitor for Recurrence
followUpActions.description: Increase monitoring for repeated denied SSH attempts from internal hosts to detect further reconnaissance or attack attempts.

postIncidentSummary.whatHappened: A firewall rule denied a TCP connection attempt from 10.0.0.240 to 10.0.0.41 on port 22, which may indicate an unauthorized SSH access attempt or reconnaissance scan.
postIncidentSummary.impactAssessment: No successful access occurred; the firewall prevented the connection. However, repeated attempts could indicate a compromised internal host or an insider threat.
postIncidentSummary.rootCause: Likely an unauthorized or misconfigured process on 10.0.0.240 attempting to access SSH on 10.0.0.41.
postIncidentSummary.lessonsLearned: Regularly review firewall logs and rules, monitor for internal reconnaissance, and ensure endpoint security controls are enforced on all internal hosts.
